What is GEO? The Complete Guide

By Jane Doe · Updated May 10, 2026

TL;DR: GEO is a content strategy that makes your pages visible to AI search engines. Research on 21,143 citations shows structured pages get 40% more visibility.

GEO (Generative Engine Optimization) is a discipline that optimizes content for AI answer engines such as ChatGPT, Perplexity and Gemini. It differs from classic SEO in 3 measurable ways.

What is GEO exactly?

GEO focuses on being cited and absorbed by AI answers. The KDD 2024 study measured up to 40% visibility gains from adding statistics, as documented at arXiv 2311.09735.

Visibility in AI answers is earned by evidence, not by keywords.

GEO by the numbers

  • 21,143 real citations analysed across 3 platforms
  • 72 page-level features correlated with absorption
  • 602 prompts tested in 2026

How does GEO differ from SEO?

SEO ranks pages; GEO gets passages quoted. The llms.txt proposal and the Vercel crawler study both shape modern practice.

AspectSEOGEO
UnitPagePassage
GoalRankCitation

Teams that publish self-contained sections with real data see the biggest gains in 2026.
